item.title: Are you kidding me?
item.content:encoded: Its literally a part of the anomaly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
saw some definite parallels to the Goa'uld and enjoyed the ride

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me or does this scp remind me of The Host? +1 btw

item.title: Soon... (Hopefully)
item.content:encoded:
No uncollapsed embedded videos on the forums, please. -Zyn
hide

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 682 is overused, just give Dr. Asma Tareen a message telling her the foundation has found alien life for her to visit. Kill two birds with one stone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
as I was reading this, I thought to myself "is this a metaphor for capitalism?" I was glad that my hunch was correct, which i figured out upon reading the first comment. :D

as a disabled person (among other things), I find this concept TERRIFYING. there would be no use for someone like me in that society, except as an organ farm.

I haven't yet read ALL of the 3000 entries, but I've read the top 10 or so, and imo, this is actually the scariest one… perhaps because, minus the beetle thing, this feels like something *that could actually happen.* through eugenics, through a fourth reich (which would of course include eugenics), or something else— well, actually, hell… it feels like maybe something that IS happening, given the capitalism metaphor. I often struggle to have any sense of self worth at all and a lot of that is because there's little i can meaningfully contribute to capitalism. hell, there are plenty of people who believe that I should allow my disability to kill me (which it eventually will without intervention) if I have to rely on welfare for that intervention (which I will, if I can qualify for it…I don't get insurance through either of my part time jobs… on the other hand, right now I make too much to qualify for medical assistance, so. *laughter of the damned*). disabled people in the same sort of predicament I'm in *do* die, and some people think it should be absolutely kosher to turn someone away from life saving care if they can't afford to pay for it. diabetics are dying because they cannot afford insulin. so… arguably eugenics is already in practice, albeit in subtle ways.

I guess that's what makes this one scarier to me than anantashesha, or red reality, or the tower… it's realistic. it's really possible. and it's arguably in process already, just through different means. I haven't read them all yet but so far this has been the scariest one for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is possibly one of my favorite SCP's, and a good example of attention to detail and amazing execution on an idea… But still in need of an editor at the end. The last moments of this article are a major shift in tone and intent of the article and were ultimately unnecessary.

I have a huge soft spot for SCP's that are sort of benign in their purpose or just forces of nature but still represent a destructive force that threatens everything. It's amazing how the concept of being moved just a single rung down the evolutionary ladder (from top dog, to, well, not) can be just as horrifying as any ghost or soul-suffers-for-eternity articles we have here… We did not need a puppet master.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They dont seem so tough, we could eat them easily

item.title: Why use humans?
item.content:encoded: Ok to be blunt this isn’t a great system that the parasite have, mostly cause they could keep a group of maybe 10 and just make the rest of humanity into blobs of fat and make the rest of the work automatic seriously there is no need to keep the human partially healthy at all and secondly how did they win a war against the rest of the humans on the planet if their host become so weak that just by tripping they are sent to the hospital seriously I think humanity would win against these with one lucky nuclear strike or by modifying a earth virus we have enough thing to kill us here it’s simple but we adapted by ourselves so the foundation should be worried just use multiple plagues at the industrial/production sector and then military action.

item.title: A Little Idea I Cooked Up As A Youtube Comment
item.content:encoded:
I mean the foundation says there's no way to get rid of the bug people.

I have a way, and it's EXTREMELY dark. But it will work. I call it 434-Attila.

Since the bugpeople don't get the concept of deception, the Foundation should pull a sneaky on them and rig votes for the Ruling Council by saying that some candidates- who are secretly agents- are objectively the best for the job, which the bugpeople would be compelled to believe, especially if presented alongside real or fake supporting evidence.

+ show block
– hide block

Once the Foundation seizes control of the society, start passing reforms that mandate that people aren't useful to society at earlier and earlier ages, slowly creeping the date of "mandatory organ donation" up until the mature population starts to decrease in numbers. Then the Ruling Council, still composed of the undercover foundation agents, should start passing mandates to decrease the number of babies allowed to be made and follow that up by increasing the number of circumstances in which a baby is to be aborted and/or terminated after birth, framing the issue as pertaining to biological defects- for example, a fictitious "allergy" to the Marce that would make the bugpeople immediately view them as a threat to society and agree to their deaths.

The ultimate goal of these measures is of course to decrease the number of bugpeople without alarming them, so this will take place over twenty years. The termination of mature bugpeople and birth-prevention of more bugpeople will need to be a rate significant enough to reduce their population to under 10 percent of its current number, 30 billion.

Once this is achieved, the population of the bugpeople will now number at ideally 3 billion, at which point the Ruling Council will fabricate a fictitious claim that the bugpeople are being targeted by a secret society other than the Foundation, particularly, one native to the bugperson planet, and that they are enemies who tricked the Ruling Council. The Ruling Council will them step down "in shame" and be replaced by a new Ruling Council, who are again foundation agents made electoral victors by the previously used method.

This new Ruling Council will recall all bugpeople living offworld back to the bugperson world and thereby end any immediate threat of bugpeople colonizing other worlds. It will then divert research funds into forming a military to combat the fictitious "rebel bugpeople" and subsequently pull funding for SCP-3003-4, thus ending long-term colonization risks.

Embedded foundation agents, who have now been living either openly or undercover varying with their missions already described in the SCP-3003 file will at this time begin framing random civilians en masse, so that a case can be made to the bugpeople for the widespread existence of the, again fabricated, rebels.

At this point open guerrilla warfare will begin, with the Ruling Council splitting into two Ruling Councils who both claim the other side is the rebellion, and that they must be stopped. Ideally this will confuse the bugpeople and they will be divided evenly into two sides which will begin a war of attrition and continue until the species 1. detonates WMDs and renders its world inhospitable, 2. drives itself to extinction, or 3. is shattered by war and mopped up by direct Foundation involvement in destroying the bugpeople world.

(If the bugpeople are split into uneven sides, steps will be taken to ensure any unevenness is eliminated via intentionally planned phyrric victories in which massive amounts of casualties will be racked up, alternatively massive "organ harvesting for the war effort" may be undertaken, a sort of war bond of blood, both of which will be cast in a positive light by the side performing it and a negative light by the side opposing them, to increase tensions, divide the two sides more, and vilify each other's sides. The end result will be a return to two equally balanced sides and the above mentioned plan may proceed uninterrupted.)


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

(p.s. This is my first time doing anything on the wiki directly so yay oof)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Surprised I hadn't upvoted this before. Deeply, profoundly unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just airmail 682 and be done with it both, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I strongly disagree with the conclusion the article reaches that Earth's defeat in a military conflict is a foregone conclusion. The writer is overlooking an important aspect of the battle. The parasites are interested in Earth and its human population as cattle. Earth and its humans are not interested in this other planet, or its inhabits, and we'd sleep better if they were all dead. The result of this is that while the parasites will have to minimize casualties not just for themselves but also for their enemy, Earth and its humans will be free to use any and all weapons of mass destruction with reckless abandon. In fact, when using them on their world, causing as much loss of life and environmental damage as possible is strongly encouraged. They're much stronger, yes, but they'll have to pull their punches while we're swinging for the fences.

I see it going down like the cold war. Mutually assured destruction will ensure that neither side intentionally starts the war. Because there will be no winner. Even if the parasites win, all they'll have won is two charred wastelands and its handful of survivors. They've got an entire world with a booming civilization right now. They will not be able to walk out of war with that. It's best for them if they simply leave Earth be. Since they're logical above all else, they'll likely see the wisdom in this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. It wasn't scary, but ultimately, SCP isn't meant to particularly BE scary. It's meant to be intriguing, and this definitely fit the bill.

Also, inb4 something simple like penicillin cures infection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this is some sort of jab at Capitalism? Yeah, no system is perfect, including Capitalism, but I feel like you failed to offer or allude to some sort of better alternate.
Which system do we have that's better than capitalism?
This was well written, but it just confused me mostly, and didn't scare me in the slightest.

item.title: Impressed
item.content:encoded: I am Impressed by this SCP. +1 for a good series IV SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I don't find this particularly scary, I do think it's very thought provoking, and works well with the dual meaning of the title.

item.title: SIMPSONS DID IT
item.content:encoded: This just feels like a retread of 752 on a larger scale, and yet somehow less alien. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh great, more bugs. Series IV is going great so far for me (surprised I didn't comment before), here's a +1. Proposals? They want proposals? Raid. Lots of it.

item.title: Great job!
item.content:encoded: I love this one. The big, elaborate planetary-wide skips are some of my favorites. Also it kinda reminds me of Unity from Rick and Morty.
Alternate title: Marce Attacks

item.title: Re: This one is the scariest SCP in my opinion.
item.content:encoded:
This adds literally nothing to the discussion, other than making you look kind of like a tryhard.

The content of your post borders on roleplay, and definitely falls square in to "not useful feedback."

The only reason this wasn't a staffpost, is I'm not sure "waxing poetic about edge-case political ideology" counts as shitcrit or not.

As a general rule, if your post deals 90% with your own feelings rather than how they related to the content of the SCP, it's not useful feedback.

item.title: This one is the scariest SCP in my opinion.
item.content:encoded:
[Irrelevant Chatter Redacted]

;) noted

item.title: Re: Neutralization Techniques
item.content:encoded: Afterthought: Nukes. If they're in giant population centres, wipe out should be easy.

item.title: Neutralization Techniques
item.content:encoded:
What if we put 682 on 3003?

Maybe involve some memetic kill agents?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1.
Sorry, but this doesn't really… hook me. Not as an SCP, at least. I read for a bit, found that the already long last tab was doubled by the commentary, and… well, I was already skimming. Honestly, this feels more like worldbuilding notes for a sci-fi novel than an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was and still is my favorite 3000 proposal.

First of all, it ties together well thought-out sci-fi and bone-chilling horror in a way that few skips are able to execute nicely. Second, the horror used really got under my skin, and left me feeling unsettled for a few days after reading.

Though I do agree with the other people here that the "elevator pitch" interview at the end is misplaced (I would personally prefer something to hammer in the horror of the whole situation), it still makes a damn fine ending.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't that the plan, that the O5 voted on? Nuke them all? I mean not literally, I think they mean to wipe SCP-3003 out using some kind of weaponized anomaly, obviously. They're just debating about actually going through with it, because of the implications (killing 30 billion people plus a whole ecosystem).

The ironic thing is that it's clear from the final addendum that the Microbes think they have the upper hand. They have no idea that the Foundation could easily wipe them all out at any time.

item.title: Re: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure that if a conversation is something someone is paying a fair bit of attention to, they won't mind checking up on a post edit. Otherwise, they wouldn't really care about notifications or not.

item.title: Re: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded: I assumed people would, like me, want the convenience of receiving all the information in their email, without having to come to the site to check if there is new information or not.

item.title: Re: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded: Flooding recent posts is a bigger deal than worrying that people won't see your edit. And for the people who have notifications enabled, wouldn't flooding a thread with your posts be an actual annoyance?

item.title: Re: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded: But then the people that got notifications enabled won't see what I write next, will they?

item.title: Re: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded:
I think it is possible that the microorganism doesn't want to modify itself because then it would acknowledge that it isn't perfect, that it isn't the end; it doesn't want a better version of itself to take it's place, it doesn't want to go extinct.

As for why it wants to avoid AI; I expect it's for similar reasons why many of the smartest people on Earth also are worried about AI development; if you make something intelligent and self-improving, it can get out of control pretty quick, and similar to the first point the microorganism doesn't want competition.

item.title: Re: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded: Just edit your existing posts instead of making repeated replies.

item.title: Re: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded: Sorry about that previous post; hit the wrong key when I was trying to delete what I wrote because I thought a little better about the situation and realized that was the wrong way to approach it. Gonna try to write what I meant to post after this.

item.title: Re: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded: (please delete this post; it was posted by accident)

item.title: Re: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded:
They keep the Marce because they're hard-wired to want to do it. As for why they don't genetically engineer the microorganism to live longer in humans… well, it's because I hadn't thought of that, but maybe they don't thoroughly understand its functionality? It's a pretty exceptional organism, and it might just be easier for them to alter humans to support them better. Not like they really mind getting implanted with marce every so often.

Before they figured out that humans have to be sufficiently old to handle marce, they had marce parasitise the babies. This turned out capital-p Poorly. I didn't bother thinking of where their babies come from, or why they don't automate things — those are just there on the whole fear-of-the-unknown principle.

item.title: I found the bio-horror creepy... But what did they do with the babies?
item.content:encoded:
I found the bio-horror creepy, as in the organ harvesting, where they don't care that the harvested are in pain…

And the parasitism…

But what did they do with the babies? And where do the babies come from??

"I know what they did to their babies until they learned they had to wait a year."

… Also, why anti-automation?

… If the microorganism makes humans so smart, why keep the Marce?

Wait… Is the microorganism actually anomalous, instead of just being utterly implausible, so it can't be altered to make it able to survive in humans and stuff?

… This is making me think of the Sarkists, 'cause biology…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Back and in general I like it, but the last bit didn't really land the punch for me. But I love the development and the slow unfolding of how wtf these bugs are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still in the middle of this and loving this. But I had to stop and comment because this:

Experimentation has shown that Earth-native humans and SCP-3003-3 are capable of producing viable offspring.

Okay then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This.

*When you write a fictional meta-critique of Capitalism but end up also meta-critiquing socialist collectivism*

I was going to write my own comment about this subtle irony, but Scorpion summed it up nicely.

Regardless, I share similar opinions to everyone else. I understand why you included the final note in service to the contest, but now that it's over I might recommend rewriting it so it isn't hitting us over the head with the plot. +1 though; I love a long, detailed article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But mostly money.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The bugs could be anything that people fixate on, really. Social status, politics, sports, personal appearance, drugs, nationalism, sex…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Super. Didn't particularly scare me as such, but very nicely written and a top notch sci fi story.

The bugs are money, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 682's adaptability allows it to be parasitised by 3003-1 so efficiently and to such an extent that 3003-3 devotes all resources to the growth and maintenance of 682. Entire population of 3003-3 attempts to feed itself to 682 to increase its biomass for the benefit of 3003-1. 682 is initially cooperative, but eventually refuses to continue consumption of 3003-3 until 3003-4 is used to eliminate Earth's entire population. 3003-3 accepts proposal, agreeing to continue to exist until these conditions are met. Foundation counter efforts are considerable and persistent, leading 3003-3 to reorient entirely around eliminating the "Hard-to-Destroy Planet". SCP-3003 entry removed and 3003-3 re-designated 1322-B.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Best case scenario - "damn reptile" will be assimilated into the collective, and will serve in distribution of 3003-1.
Worst case - Collective will be assimilated by her and will learn to serve her needs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nuke them. Nuke the planet, nuke the star, nuke everything. Nuke Earth if you have to. Shove that damn reptile through the portal, send Abel too. Stop this thing from spreading at all costs, we don't have to win, we just have to make sure they lose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure. I distinctly recall writing large chunks of the SCP without any spaces between words or paragraphs, but at some point before I uploaded this to the mainsite, they were added.

My guess? Social justice sorcerers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is with the fucking spacing?

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded:
All in all that was in pretty poor taste.

Apologies, this was not in any way my intention. I will refrain from making such jokes in the future.

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded:
Yes, we know. Of course that's not what you said, because you wouldn't say skrub in an official staff post.

It's evident to you, but it paints Zyn in a very negative light to new members who don't know you or Zyn and have no idea you're joking. All in all that was in pretty poor taste.

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded:
Uh… I'm assuming you're joking here, given that the official warning was issued because the feedback you gave was repeatedly problematic?

Yes. I ruined things for myself because I was too stupid to wait until I understood how serious this community was. I was joking.

Apologies for taking this discussion off rails.

I am interested in trying to write a tale inspired off of this scip. (Likely the journal of a researcher who got lost on the planet/some really disturbing story involving D-Class.) If I have to ask communism about this, I would like to do so now.

Am I permitted to create a tale about this article?

If you need to contact me for further information on the tale such as the plot, you can contact me via discord.

Name: ARISTOTSKAN SODAPOP COMPANY
Tag: #3236

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded:
But I am determined to do so, because I absolutely need my job back.

Uh… I'm assuming you're joking here, given that the official warning was issued because the feedback you gave was repeatedly problematic?

EDIT: wait, how did you even remember the exact thread?

Staff keeps records. I consulted those records.

Anyway, this discussion is getting off-track from the article at hand, so if anyone would like additional information, please send me a PM.

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded:
For those who are interested, this is what I said:

Yes, we know. Of course that's not what you said, because you wouldn't say skrub in an official staff post. was a rough start for me. Back when I had no idea how the SCP Foundation worked, and had most of my information from Deviant Art posts. It's been months since that happened, and I still haven't written an article. But I am determined to do so, because I absolutely need my job back.

EDIT: wait, how did you even remember the exact thread?

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded:
If you give me permission, I will certainly try, because once Zyn told me

You little skrub. Your critiques are pretty bad. Im only letting you write critiques if you have a successful main site article

(Quote may be modified in some way)

For those who are interested, this is what I said: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-2073956/just-an-idea-for-an-scp-and-i-m-asking-for-feedback#post-2733304

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded:
I would try to make a tale, but I don't believe I am competent enough to do it successfully.

If you give me permission, I will certainly try, because once Zyn told me

You little skrub. Your critiques are pretty bad. Im only letting you write critiques if you have a successful main site article

(Quote may be modified in some way)

And I want to be able to give critique. So if I'm going to make an article as my first successful main site post, it's going to be a tale for this.

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded:
Couldn't care less about making tales about this. Would love to see someone else take a crack at it, though.

Given that the SCP mainlist is home to a startling array of scopes, genres, and subject matters, I'm not sure what renders this article uniquely unsuitable for a place on it.

As for getting its own series, there's no reason that series couldn't theoretically be hosted on this site, being anchored to the Foundationverse proper by 3003's presence on the mainlist.

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded: Me too, because this seems like something worth expanding on.

item.title: Re: -1 for SCPness, +20 for opening doors to creativity.
item.content:encoded: oh, sorry. communismwillwin has made some pretty good tales, so i hope he makes one on this soon.
